what: Recover the true v089 baseline before resuming tuning
why: The recent v149-v164 line benchmarks a branch that fails server-side correctness. Restoring the recorded best_submission implementation re-establishes a valid baseline before testing any more shape-specific routing changes.

what: Use a16w8 page_size=16 only for bs=32,kv=1024 on the true v089 baseline
why: The earlier bs32 page-size idea was evaluated only on the later invalid v149+ line. Retesting it on the recovered v089 baseline isolates whether the medium 1k shape can benefit from fewer pages without touching the known-sensitive bs=64 route or any 8k shape.
override: A similar shape idea was queued on top of the invalid v149+ branch; this reruns it from the recovered valid v089 baseline.

what: Route only bs=256,kv=1024 to bf16_persist page_size=128 on the true v089 baseline
why: The heaviest 1k shape may benefit from cutting metadata and page count, while the smaller 1k shapes stay on the validated a16w8 route. Running this from the recovered v089 baseline avoids the server-correctness problems in the later v149+ branch.
override: A related bs256 1k idea was explored on the invalid v149+ line; this reruns it from the recovered valid v089 baseline.

what: Use bf16_persist page_size=128 only for bs=64,kv>=4096 on the true v089 baseline
why: Earlier work suggested the large-page benefit on 8k likely came from bs=64 rather than bs=256, but the clean bs64-only branch was quota-blocked instead of measured. This keeps every 1k shape and the other 8k batches on the recovered v089 routing while isolating the page-size change to the medium-large 8k case.
override: This retries the previously blocked bs64-only 8k page-size experiment from the recovered valid v089 baseline.

what: Use the repo reference-style a8w8 persistent MLA kernel as the new correctness anchor
why: The archived v089/v165 hybrid passes the narrow test gate but fails benchmark on additional fixed seeds, and v166 shows the same failure pattern. The bundled reference quantizes Q to fp8 and uses page_size=1 with fast_mode=False, so it is the closest available implementation to the checked reference path.
override: This replaces the stale archived baseline after repeated benchmark-seed correctness failures.
